channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump struggles with independent and moderate Republican support ahead of 2026 midterms
item.description: President Donald Trump is facing declining support among key voter groups, raising concerns about the potential impact on the 2026 midterm elections. Political analyst Paul Rieckhoff, host of the 'Ind...

item.title: Take-Two projects $8.1 billion revenue for FY27, largely driven by GTA 6 launch
item.description: Take-Two Interactive is forecasting record revenue for fiscal year 2027, largely fueled by the upcoming release of Grand Theft Auto 6. The company expects net revenue between $7.9 and $8.1 billion, wi...

item.title: Mitochondrial l-2-Hydroxyglutarate Acts as a Physiological Signaling Molecule with Functional Roles
item.description: A recent study published in Nature investigates the role of l-2-hydroxyglutarate (l-2-HG), a metabolite traditionally considered toxic, in normal physiological processes. The researchers found that l-...

item.title: West Bengal Government Requires Prior Approval for Officials to Communicate with Media
item.description: The newly formed Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) government in West Bengal has issued an order restricting state government officials from speaking to the media or sharing information without prior permi...

item.title: How French Philosophers Influenced the Rise of Woke Ideology in the West
item.description: In this opinion piece, Brivael Le Pogam argues that modern 'wokism' can be traced back to French intellectual movements, particularly the works of Michel Foucault, Jacques Derrida, and Gilles Deleuze....

item.title: Tackling Maternal Mortality: Preventing Deaths from Postpartum Haemorrhage
item.description: Maternal deaths remain a serious concern in Kenya, especially from preventable causes like postpartum haemorrhage (PPH). According to the Ministry of Health, the country loses 15 mothers, 92 newborns,...

item.title: Protests erupt in Nuuk as U.S. opens first consulate in Greenland since 1950s
item.description: The inauguration of a new U.S. consulate in Nuuk, Greenland, has prompted public demonstrations amid local concerns over American influence on the island. This marks the first permanent U.S. diplomati...

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's England World Cup Squad: Foden, Palmer and Maguire Omitted
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is expected to name his 26-man England squad on Friday for the 2026 World Cup, which will be held across the United States, Mexico, and Canada. According to reports, several notable play...

item.title: Philippine health leaders push for stronger maternal and child vaccination programs
item.description: Local government units (LGUs) and healthcare specialists in the Philippines are urging a more robust approach to maternal and child immunization due to the ongoing threat of vaccine-preventable diseas...

item.title: Canada-U.S. Trade Talks Stall as Poilievre Launches Alberta Campaign for Unity
item.description: On May 21, 2026, Canada-U.S. trade negotiations remain stalled, with no progress reported on easing tensions or the upcoming review of the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement, leaving business leaders and po...

item.title: Africa’s Development Depends on Strong Partnerships Across Sectors
item.description: Africa’s development cannot be driven by governments, markets or donors alone. The 25th anniversary of Nepad highlights that partnership is central to African renewal. African ownership, regional inte...

item.title: UN Launches Investigation After Death of Rwanda Genocide Suspect in Detention
item.description: The United Nations International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals has initiated a thorough investigation into the death of Félicien Kabuga, a high-profile suspect in the 1994 Rwanda genocide,...

item.title: Spotify Launches Feature to Reserve Concert Tickets for Top Fans
item.description: Spotify has announced a new feature called 'Reserved' that aims to make concert ticket purchasing fairer for dedicated fans. Starting this summer in the U.S., Premium subscribers who are identified as...

item.title: Dwight Ramos Eager to Introduce Japanese B.League Basketball Culture to Filipino Fans in Manila
item.description: Dwight Ramos, a notable Filipino basketball player with experience in Japan's B.League, expressed excitement about sharing the unique culture and style of the league with Filipino fans during the upco...

item.title: Nacho López’s 1953 Photo Series Captures Maty Huitrón and Everyday Life in Mexico City
item.description: In January 1953, Mexican photographer Nacho López conducted a unique social and cultural experiment featuring actress Maty Huitrón. He asked Huitrón to go to the market while he pretended to leave to ...

item.title: Kadri Hamzat Wins Lagos APC Governorship Primary, Set for 2027 Election
item.description: Lagos State Deputy Governor, Kadri Obafemi Hamzat, don win the All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship primary election, wey go make am the party candidate for 2027 Lagos governorship election. F...

item.title: KZN Overhauls NPO Funding System to Improve Transparency and Support for Vulnerable Communities
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal's Social Development MEC, Mbali Shinga, has announced a major revamp of the province’s non-profit organisation (NPO) funding system. The changes aim to restore transparency, accountabili...

item.title: Sudan faces severe health system collapse amid ongoing conflict
item.description: The health situation in Sudan is very serious after three years of intense conflict. Hospitals and clinics have been damaged or destroyed, leaving millions without basic healthcare. Many medical cente...

item.title: Ronaldo Leads Al Nassr to First Saudi Pro League Championship Since 2019
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo don show him class again as e score two important goals wey help Al Nassr clinch the Saudi Pro League title on Thursday with a convincing 4-1 win over Damac. This win mark the first ...

item.title: Six DPWH Officials and Contractors Charged Over Non-Existent Davao Farm-to-Market Roads Worth P94 Million
item.description: MANILA — Six officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) in Davao Occidental, along with several private contractors, have been formally charged over eight alleged non-existent f...
